rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LOCKE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rex Moore Electrical Contractors and Engineers, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is continued to March 3, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 per the CCP 437c(h) request of plaintiff It appears from the declaration of Ms. Barekatain that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot now be presented, namely testing results on the refrigeration panel. Substantive opposition, if any, to be filed and served by February 21. Reply papers, if any, to be filed and served by February 28. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARMICHAEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant McJunkin Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production because its separate statement does not cite to facts showing that it propounded all facts, witnesses and documents interrogatories on plaintiff, and that plaintiff¿s responses thereto were factually devoid. Even if defendant had done so, there is a triable issue whether plaintiff used, installed and/or removed gaskets supplied by defendant. Plaintiff¿s objection to the second sentence in paragraph 6 of Mr. Colonna¿s declaration is sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARMICHAEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Monterey Mechanical Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. By failing to cite to plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special discovery in its separate statement, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD GRIST VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS'B*P' AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant ABHI-Crockett, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the laborers he identified were employed by defendant or that they disturbed asbestos-containing insulation. Mr. Grist¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Moreover, Mr. Grist¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether the insulation debris swept up by defendant¿s laborers in his presence contained asbestos. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Grist¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD GRIST VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS'B*P' AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary judgment is off calendar as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (strict liability, false representation and punitive damges) and denied as to issue 4 (duty). Defendant concedes that plaintiff created a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing joint compound attributable to defendant. Dr. Richard Cohen¿s declaration creates a triable issues whether defendant had constructive knowledge that the drywall products it worked with in plaintiff¿s presence in the 1960s contained asbestos and whether defendant had constructive knowledge of the health hazards of asbestos. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability, false representation and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HOFF et al VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 13. Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion to stay for forum non conveniens is denied without prejudice to the motion being renewed in the event that all of the defendants sued for exposures occurring in California are dismissed or granted summary judgment. Based on plaintiff¿s representation that six of the nine remaining defendants have been sued for alleged exposures occurring in California and/or in connection with Mr. Hoff¿s service in the Navy and the lack of significant evidence that defendants would be seriously inconvenienced by this case remaining in California, the public and private factors do not favor granting the motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE SEEVERS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 15. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to punitive damages and off calendar as to false representation. Mr. Walter¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Seevers was exposed to Kaiser Gypsum joint compound. Defendant failed to satisfy the burden of production as to asbestos content of that joint compound due to the reference in plaintiffs¿ interrogatory responses to defendant¿s General Order 129 interrogatory responses. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence warranting the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs¿ special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on this issue. Plaintiffs dismissed their false representation cause of action. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Walters and to its General Order interrogatory responses are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ERNEST STONE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 16. Defendant Federal-Mogul Asbestos Personal Injury Trust¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue a) whether any Fel-Pro gaskets he may have installed or disturbed contained asbestos and b) whether any exposure that may have resulted increased his risk of disease. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA PHILLIPS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 17. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ complaint contains sufficient allegations that Mr. Phillips died from an asbestos-related illness. The absence of any allegation as to date of decedent¿s death does not render the first amended complaint deficient as a matter of law. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA PHILLIPS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Show Cause Why Sanctions Shall Not Be Imposed For Failure To Serve Summons On Complaint For Survival, Wrongful Death Â¿¿ Asbestos In A Timely Manner
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 3, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 17. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s request for the court to issue an order to show cause why sanctions shall not be imposed for failure to timely serve summons on defendant with the wrongful death complaint is denied. The court exercises its discretion not to hold a collateral hearing on this issue given that plaintiffs were tardy only by four days and defendant has not shown that it has been prejudiced by that tardiness. Order signed in open court. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BERRY & BERRY, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION VS. GABRIELLE JACKSON et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 20. Defendant Gabriel Jackson¿s demurrer to first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The first amended complaint purports to allege causes of action for breach of contract and common counts, but no attachments on these purported causes of action were attached. The first purported cause of action for ¿intentional tort¿ appears to be a claim for conversion (civil theft), but that is not stated explicitly. The third purported cause of action for breach of trust is not a cognizable claim. Nor is it clear whether a general unspecified claim for intentional tort is sought to be alleged. For guidance of the parties in the future, Ms. Jackson is subject to a suit where it is alleged that she personally committed misconduct, apart from any misconduct that may have been committed by others that are attributable to Jackson & Wallace. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: COMPLEX ASBESTOS LITIGATION*
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recover Unreimbursed Costs And Fees Against The Clients Of Jackson, Jenkins & Renstrom, Etc.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 3, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 21. Berry & Berry¿s motion to recover unreimbursed costs and fees against the clients of Jackson, Jenkins & Renstrom is granted in part. The clients of Jackson Jenkins Renstrom LLP listed on Exhibit A to the ¿status¿ report dated January 31, 2011 must pay the amounts listed next to their names no later than March 5, 2011 to the extent that payment for all of some of those amounts have not already been paid to Berry & Berry by or on behalf of those clients. All other aspects of the motion are denied as not having been shown to be necessary to accomplish the purposes of General Order 41, but may be made in the future upon a fuller showing of need. = (220/HEK)
